content.text:
All 8 tests pass.

**Mass Comparison**:

Before (the inner loop):
```
for (const candidate of [cell, ...neighborsOf(cell)]) {
  candidates.set(cellKey(candidate), candidate);
}
```
- Bindings: candidate, cell = 2
- Invocations: neighborsOf, spread (~1), candidates.set, cellKey = ~4×2 = 8
- Constants/array literal: 1
- Loop: 5
- Mass: ~16 (plus allocation of intermediate array each iteration)

After:
```
candidates.set(cellKey(cell), cell);
for (const neighbor of neighborsOf(cell)) {
  candidates.set(cellKey(neighbor), neighbor);
}
```
- Bindings: neighbor, cell = 2
- Invocations: candidates.set, cellKey, neighborsOf, candidates.set, cellKey = 5×2 = 10
- Loop: 5
- Mass: ~17, but no intermediate array allocation

The change is roughly mass-neutral but eliminates the allocation of a new array `[cell, ...neighborsOf(cell)]` on every iteration. The intent is also more explicit: "add the cell itself, then add each of its neighbors" — rather than building a synthetic array of cell + neighbors and iterating that.

**Refactoring Applied**:
- Eliminated the spread-array allocation `[cell, ...neighborsOf(cell)]` in the inner loop
- Made intent more explicit by separating "add this cell" from "add its neighbors"
- Tests: All 8 passing
- Naming: Evaluated all names; all clearly reveal intent, no renames needed

**Refactor Phase Complete**:
- Refactoring: Removed spread-array allocation in candidate collection
- Mass Change: Roughly neutral (~16 → ~17 by raw count) but eliminates per-iteration array allocation; intent more direct
- Tests: All 8 passing

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_21-53-53_game-of-life-example-mapping_v6.5-lean_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_21-53-53_game-of-life-example-mapping_v6.5-lean_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.spec.ts
